blocks.title: We are empowering the next generation of founders in karlsruhe to turn ideas into impact
In 5 years our members are impactful entre|preneurs, contributing to Karlsruhe’s ecosystem.

blocks.content:
### 2008: Founding at KIT

A group of students — Moritz Meenen, Jan Henric Stönner, Martin Mitscher, Tobias Griesshaber, and Maximilian Engelken — founded the new university group "PionierGarage" at KIT. Regular Tuesday meetings became the main activity, with founders invited for "Meet-the-Founder" sessions so students could get first-hand entrepreneurship experience.



### 2009: Partnership with Cyberforum

Kerstin Weber-Sanguigno joined the association, and the partnership with CyberForum began. As the biggest player in the Karlsruhe startup scene, CyberForum connected PionierGarage with founders and mentors and embedded it more deeply in the local ecosystem.



### 2011: First Startup Tour to Silicon Valley

The first Startup Tour took members to Silicon Valley for two weeks to experience the local founder scene. Startup Tours have been an annual tradition ever since, with new destinations each year.



### 2013: GROW is born

First edition of the GROW Gründungswettbewerb, supported by Holger Kujath (founder of knuddels). The competition has been held every year at KIT since, with growing audience and team numbers. Holger Kujath continues to support GROW each year as a donor and sponsor.



### 2016: Launchpad opens

Opening of the PionierGarage Launchpad — the first student-run incubator in Germany. After a year of planning, the Launchpad offered startups affordable workspace with strong proximity to the student body. Official move from AStA at KIT to the association's own premises. Ressorts and regular Ressort meetings were introduced, and active membership has grown continuously since.



### 2019: 10-year anniversary of PionierGarage

GROW Final drew 500 attendees at the Audimax. First PionierWochenende. Celebration of the 10-year anniversary of PionierGarage. Ressort structure was replaced with a project-based structure.



### 2024: Relocation to CUBE

Move-out from the Launchpad due to the building's poor condition. Relocation to the CUBE at KIT-Gründerschmiede



### 2026: Grow reaching the 1000 visitors benchmark

First time in history that GROW is reaching 1000 visitors incuding Daniel Jung, Investors and mentors from all over Germany
